title: CIL Industrial Engineering — Advanced Mock 11 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.solution: B

questions.stem: Find the next: 2, 11, 38, 197, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To steal someone's thunder':
options: To take credit for another's idea
questions.solution: To take credit for another's idea

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's installed nuclear power capacity is:
questions.solution: 7.5 GW

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To burn the midnight oil':
options: To work late into the night
questions.solution: To work late into the night

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The word 'abundant' in the passage most nearly means:
questions.solution: Plentiful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To read between the lines':
questions.solution: To understand the hidden meaning

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

The PLI scheme is aimed at:
questions.solution: Reducing dependence on solar imports

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To hit the nail on the head':
questions.solution: To be exactly right

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

Coal accounts for about what percentage of India's installed capacity?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To let the cat out of the bag':
questions.solution: To reveal a secret unintentionally

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Coal is the most abundant fossil fuel in India and plays a crucial role in the country's energy mix. India has the fourth-largest coal reserves in the world, with an estimated 344 billion tonnes. Coal India Limited (CIL) is the largest coal-producing company globally, accounting for about 82% of India's coal output. However, concerns about environmental degradation and climate change have prompted a shift towards cleaner energy sources like solar and wind power. The government has set a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

The government's renewable energy target by 2030 is ____ GW.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'A wild goose chase':
questions.solution: A useless pursuit

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the late 18th century, was powered largely by coal. Steam engines, fuelled by coal, drove factories, railways, and ships, transforming economies and societies. The demand for coal led to the expansion of mining operations across Europe and America. However, the social cost was high, with poor working conditions, child labour, and environmental pollution. The legacy of the Industrial Revolution continues to influence modern industrial practices and environmental policies. Today, many former mining regions are being revitalised through diversification and clean energy initiatives.

What powered steam engines during the Industrial Revolution?
questions.solution: Coal

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To burn one's bridges':
questions.solution: To eliminate all options

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The word 'mandatory' in the passage most nearly means:
questions.solution: Compulsory

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be a dark horse':
questions.solution: An unexpected winner

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's energy demand is expected to grow at ____ % annually.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To bite off more than one can chew':
options: To take on too much responsibility
questions.solution: To take on too much responsibility

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
options: The sceneries of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains have been breathtaking.
questions.solution: The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PRUDENT:
questions.solution: Reckless

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To take with a grain of salt':
questions.solution: To be skeptical

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The data is being analysed.
options: The data has been being analysed.
questions.solution: The data are being analysed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To beat around the bush':
questions.solution: To avoid the main topic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I prefer coffee than tea.
questions.solution: I prefer coffee to tea.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of JUBILANT:
questions.solution: Gloomy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of RESILIENT:
questions.solution: Tough

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bottleneck detection:
options: Workstation with highest utilization. Determines system throughput. TOC identifies
questions.solution: Bottleneck: utilization=100% or max. Shifting bottleneck. Capacity constraint resource (CCR).

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Process Capability Index Cpk uses:
questions.solution: Cpk = min[(USL-μ)/(3σ), (μ-LSL)/(3σ)]. Accounts for centering. Cp = (USL-LSL)/(6σ) ignores centering.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ALDEP (Automated Layout Design):
options: Construction algorithm. Sweep width. Placement based on relationship chart. Random start
questions.solution: ALDEP: builds layout step by step. Compute score. Best of multiple random starts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anthropometry: design for 5th percentile female:
options: Ensures 95% of female population can use (reaches, fits)
questions.solution: Design: reach → 5th percentile female. Strength → 5th percentile male. Clearance → 95th percentile.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Quick Response Manufacturing (QRM) reduces:
options: Lead time throughout the entire enterprise
questions.solution: QRM: Rajan. Enterprise-wide lead time reduction. Cells, QRM zones, codename scheduling.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Value Analysis: functional analysis focuses on:
questions.solution: Value Analysis: define function in verb-noun pairs. Brainstorm alternatives. Evaluate by function, not component.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maintenance types:
questions.solution: Breakdown: reactive. Preventive: time-based. Predictive: vibration, thermography. RCM: reliability-centered.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: PAC (Production Activity Control):
questions.solution: PAC: real-time shop floor control. Dispatching, monitoring, expediting. Executes MRP/CRP plans.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quantity discount model: compare total cost at each price break EOQ:
options: Minimum total cost may not be at the lowest unit price
questions.solution: Evaluate TC at each price break. Optimal may be between breaks if holding cost changes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Component A (R = 0.95) is in series with a parallel pair B (0.85) and C (0.9). The system reliability is about:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Chaos theory in management suggests:
options: Small changes can have large unpredictable effects
questions.solution: Chaos: sensitive dependence on initial conditions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹50000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Capital recovery factor converts:
options: Present value to equivalent annual cost
questions.solution: CRF = i(1+i)^n / ((1+i)^n - 1). Used for annualized cost of capital investments. Standard engineering economy tool.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast — bias vs accuracy:
options: Bias: systematic error (average of errors). Accuracy: MAD, MSE, MAPE. Unbiased = zero average error
questions.solution: Bias: TS detects. Persistent bias → adjust model. Accuracy: measures magnitude regardless of sign.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Computerized layout — CRAFT:
options: Heuristic: pairwise exchange of departments to minimize material handling cost (rectilinear distance)
questions.solution: CRAFT: improvement algorithm. Quadratic assignment problem. Distance: centroid-to-centroid (rectilinear).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics (human factors) is mainly concerned with:
options: Fitting the worker to the machine only
Fitting the job/workplace to the worker
questions.solution: Ergonomics designs tasks and workplaces around human capabilities to improve safety and efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — 40-hour workshop:
options: Structured 5-day job plan. Multi-disciplinary team. 15-30% cost savings typical
questions.solution: Workshop: pre-study, workshop, post-study. Implementation phase. Savings verification.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: CRAFT (Computerized Relative Allocation of Facilities):
options: Improves layout by swapping department locations
questions.solution: CRAFT: iterative improvement. Swaps departments to reduce material handling cost. Uses from-to data.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Total float formula:
options: LS minus ES equals LF minus EF
questions.solution: Total float: LS-ES or LF-EF. Without delaying project.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Monte Carlo simulation uses:
questions.solution: Monte Carlo: generate random inputs from distributions. Compute outputs. Build output distribution. Estimate risk.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SMED (quick changeover):
options: Single-digit minute setup. Internal (machine stopped) vs external (while running)
questions.solution: SMED: Shingo. Convert internal to external. Streamline elements. 75-95% reduction.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Bathtub curve: useful life period has:
questions.solution: Bathtub: infant mortality (decreasing λ), useful life (constant λ), wear-out (increasing λ). Exponential in middle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total landed cost (TLC):
options: Product cost + freight + duties + insurance + warehousing + overhead
questions.solution: TLC: true cost of sourcing. Includes hidden costs. Used for make-vs-buy and sourcing decisions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In CPM/PERT, the critical path is the:
questions.solution: The critical path's length sets the minimum project duration; its activities have zero float.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Customers arrive at 15/hour and spend on average 0.75 hour in the system. The average number in the system is:
questions.solution: Little's law: L = λ·W = 15×0.75 = 11.25.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Warehouse automation: AS/RS provides:
questions.solution: AS/RS: cranes, shuttles, carousel systems. High density, fast throughput, accuracy. High capital, low labor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FAST (Function Analysis System Technique):
options: Shows logical relationships between functions using how-why questioning
questions.solution: FAST: higher-order functions (left) to lower-order (right). Critical path shows essential functions.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Andon system in lean manufacturing provides:
options: Visual signal for help or problem detection
questions.solution: Andon: signal lights/boards. Worker pulls cord to signal problem. Team leader responds. Stops line if needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: From-to chart: high flow between departments A and B means:
options: A and B should be placed close together
questions.solution: From-to: flow matrix. High flow = proximity. Low flow can be distant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kanban: number of kanbans = DL(1+α)/C. For D=100/day, L=2 days, α=0.1, C=50:
questions.solution: Kanban: D=demand rate, L=lead time, α=safety factor, C=container size. Round up.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 600 units using 40 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:
questions.solution: Productivity = output/input = 600/40 = 15 units/hour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 3+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2, tm = 5, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+7, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 4+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 3+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 10, 1-3-4 = 13. The critical path is the longest = 13 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4, tm = 6, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Allowances: personal needs allowance typically:
questions.solution: Personal: 5-7%. Fatigue: 0-40% (depends on task). Delay: 0-5% (unavoidable).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Global sourcing — challenges:
options: Longer lead time, currency risk, quality, IP, logistics, culture, regulations
questions.solution: Global sourcing: total landed cost. Incoterms. Countertrade. Duty/tariff optimization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facility location factors:
options: Proximity to customers, suppliers, transportation, labor, utilities, community factors
questions.solution: Location: factor rating, center of gravity, break-even analysis. Weighted score.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 140, 150 and 160 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Green supply chain:
options: Reduce environmental impact. Reverse logistics. Carbon footprint. Circular economy
questions.solution: Green: sustainable sourcing, eco-design, closed-loop. Life cycle assessment. Carbon reporting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Daily demand = 80, lead time = 4 days, safety stock = 100 units. The reorder point is:
questions.solution: ROP = (demand × lead time) + safety stock = 80×4 + 100 = 420 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 3, tm = 5, tp = 12 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequencing — LPT:
options: Longest Processing Time. Creates large jobs early → delays small jobs. Used for balancing
questions.solution: LPT: often worst. But can be useful for machine loading balance. EDD better for due dates.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Assignment problem is a special case of:
questions.solution: Assignment: one-to-one matching. Special case where all supply=demand=1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 8 min with 10% allowances. Its standard time is:
questions.solution: Standard time = normal × (1 + allowance) = 8×(1 + 10/100) = 8.8 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹120, variable cost ₹60/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹20000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Six Sigma:
options: 3.4 DPMO. DMAIC (Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control). DMADV (Design)
questions.solution: Six Sigma: process shifted ±1.5σ. σ-level = (USL-μ)/σ or (μ-LSL)/σ. 6σ ≈ 2 ppb per tail.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE):
options: A × P × Q. Availability = uptime/total time. Performance = actual/theoretical. Quality = good/total
questions.solution: Six big losses: breakdown, setup, idling, reduced speed, defects, startup losses (link to OEE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Factory physics — Little's law:
options: WIP = TH × CT. Throughput × Cycle Time. Predicts system performance
questions.solution: Little: applicable to any stable system. WIP: work-in-process. TH: output rate. CT: time in system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.3, last actual = 200 and last forecast = 180, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Economic Production Quantity (EPQ): Q_p = √(2DS/(H(1-d/p))). For p=50/day, d=20/day:
options: Q_p is larger than EOQ (finite replenishment)
questions.solution: EPQ: production rate p > demand d. Factor (1-d/p) < 1, so Q_p > EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast horizon:
options: Short-term (<3 mo), Medium (3-24 mo), Long-term (>2 yr). Accuracy decreases with horizon
questions.solution: Short-term: operational (MRP). Medium: tactical (capacity). Long-term: strategic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Johnson's rule (2 machines):
options: Minimizes makespan. Sequence: smallest processing time; if on M1 → start, if on M2 → end
questions.solution: Johnson: n jobs, 2 machines. Optimal sequence. For >2 machines: Jackson's extension.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast horizon: accuracy decreases as horizon increases because:
options: More uncertainty accumulates over longer future periods
questions.solution: Forecast error increases with horizon. Short-term: operational. Long-term: strategic planning only.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Manufacturing cell design:
options: U-shaped cells. Multi-skilled operators. Reduced WIP, quick changeovers
questions.solution: Cell: one-piece flow. SMED: single-minute exchange of dies. Takt time = available time/demand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecasting — new products:
options: Analogous products, market research (conjoint), diffusion models (Bass)
questions.solution: Bass model: innovators + imitators. p: innovation coefficient. q: imitation coefficient. Peak at t*.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bill of Materials (BOM):
options: Product structure — all components and quantities to make one unit
questions.solution: BOM: single-level, multi-level, indented, modular. Used by MRP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tracking signal:
options: TS = Σ(Running error)/MAD. Monitor forecast bias. ±4 = out of control
questions.solution: TS: if outside ±4, forecast is biased. Need to adjust model (α, trend, or method).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequencing — CR:
options: Critical Ratio = (due date - now)/(remaining processing time). CR < 1: behind
questions.solution: CR: dynamic, adjusts to remaining time. CR = 1: on schedule. CR < 1: expedite. CR > 1: ahead.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Exponential smoothing with trend (Holt method): level alpha=0.3, trend beta=0.2:
questions.solution: Holt: level estimate = weighted average of current demand and previous level+trend.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 4000, S = ₹50, H = ₹4), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×4000×50/4) ≈ 316.2 units, where the two cost curves cross.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="240" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M40 25 C 90 90 110 100 240 112" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="60" y="40" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">ordering</text><line x1="35" y1="118" x2="235" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="190" y="55" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">holding</text><path d="M40 60 C 110 30 120 30 235 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">total cost</text><line x1="120" y1="48" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="110" y="134" font-size="8" fill="#475569">Q*</text></svg>
figure.caption: Inventory total-cost curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR):
options: Total repair time / Number of repairs
questions.solution: MTTR: average downtime per repair. Lower = faster repair. Includes diagnosis + fix + test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARIMA autocorrelation function (ACF):
options: Correlation between series and lagged version. Decay for AR. Cutoff for MA
questions.solution: ACF: for MA(q): cuts off after lag q. For AR(p): tails off. Partial ACF: cuts off for AR after p.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reorder point (ROP):
options: R = d·L + SS (demand during lead time + safety stock)
questions.solution: ROP: fixed quantity system (Q-system). Continuous review. Lead time demand distribution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traveling salesman: nearest neighbor heuristic:
options: Start at city 1, always go to nearest unvisited city, return to start
questions.solution: Nearest neighbor: O(n²). Quick but suboptimal. Tour quality: typically 25% worse than optimal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chi-square test:
options: Goodness-of-fit, independence, homogeneity. For categorical data. χ² = Σ(O-E)²/E
questions.solution: χ²: expected ≥ 5 per cell. Yates correction for 2×2. Cramer's V for strength of association.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 100, 110 and 160 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 3 and tp = 14 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow process chart:
options: Sequence of operations, transports, inspections, delays, storage
questions.solution: Flow chart: man type (worker activities), material type (material movements), equipment type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory — anticipation (seasonal) stock:
options: Build in advance of peak demand. Level production strategy
questions.solution: Anticipation: pre-building. Decoupling: between production stages. Hedge: price speculation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A weighted moving average differs from a simple moving average by:
options: Assigning different weights to different periods
questions.solution: Weighted MA typically gives more weight to recent periods, improving responsiveness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period model (newsvendor):
options: Service level = Cu/(Cu+Co). Cu = underage cost, Co = overage cost
questions.solution: Newsvendor: balance risk of overstocking vs understocking. Perishable goods, seasonal items.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the inventory cost curve shown (D = 2000, S = ₹50, H = ₹5), the order size Q* that minimizes total cost (where ordering and holding costs are equal) is:
questions.solution: Total cost is minimum at the EOQ Q* = √(2DS/H) = √(2×2000×50/5) ≈ 200 units, where the two cost curves cross.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="240" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M40 25 C 90 90 110 100 240 112" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="60" y="40" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">ordering</text><line x1="35" y1="118" x2="235" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="190" y="55" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">holding</text><path d="M40 60 C 110 30 120 30 235 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="120" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">total cost</text><line x1="120" y1="48" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="110" y="134" font-size="8" fill="#475569">Q*</text></svg>
figure.caption: Inventory total-cost curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 30 units/day with a lead time of 3 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 30×3 = 90 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Game theory — Dominance:
options: Remove dominated strategies (worse than another in all outcomes). Simplify the game
questions.solution: Dominance: row i dominates row k if a_ij ≥ a_kj for all j (max). Column: vice versa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linear programming: dual variable value y_i represents:
options: Shadow price of i-th constraint — change in optimal Z per unit increase in b_i
questions.solution: Dual: y_i = ∂Z*/∂b_i. Shadow price: value of relaxing one unit of constraint i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing: M/M/c system utilization ρ = λ/(cμ). For stability:
options: ρ < 1 (average arrival rate < total service rate of c servers)
questions.solution: Multi-server: ρ = λ/(cμ). Must be <1. Average time in system: W = f(ρ,c)/μ.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Game theory saddle point exists when:
questions.solution: Saddle point: maximin = minimax. Pure strategy Nash equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 50 units/day with a lead time of 7 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 50×7 = 350 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 40 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 10 min. Its balance efficiency is:
questions.solution: Efficiency = Σt/(N·C) = 40/(5×10) = 80%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which comparison of system reliability is correct?
options: A parallel arrangement is more reliable than the same components in series
Series is more reliable than parallel
questions.solution: Series reliability is the product (lower than any part); parallel redundancy raises reliability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Leagile supply chain:
options: Leagile: lean upstream (efficient), agile downstream (responsive). Decoupling point
questions.solution: Leagile: combined lean + agile. Forecast-driven upstream, demand-driven downstream.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Decision Tree Analysis: Expected Monetary Value at chance nodes:
questions.solution: EMV: Σ(Pi × Vi). Decision nodes: choose branch with highest EMV. Incorporates uncertainty into decisions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simplex tableau: entering variable selected by most negative (z_j - c_j) for:
questions.solution: Max: most negative reduced cost enters. Min: most positive. Bland's rule: smallest index to avoid cycling.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Reliability growth model: Duane model assumes:
options: Cumulative MTBF improves as power function of time
questions.solution: Duane: MTBF_cumulative = aT^b. Log-linear relationship. b is growth rate. Used during development testing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 20 units/day with a lead time of 5 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 20×5 = 100 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the break-even chart shown (fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹80, variable cost ₹60/unit), the marked point where revenue meets total cost occurs at:
questions.solution: BEP = FC/(p − v) = 20000/(80 − 60) = 1000 units, where total revenue = total cost.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="240" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="80" x2="240" y2="80" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="180" y="76" font-size="8" fill="#475569">total cost</text><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="220" y2="25" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="180" y="40" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">revenue</text><circle cx="120" cy="62" r="4" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="120" y1="62" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="110" y="134" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">BEP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Break-even chart

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 30 units/day with a lead time of 7 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 30×7 = 210 units.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: JIT (Just-in-Time) goals include:
options: Zero waste, zero defects, zero inventory
questions.solution: JIT: produce what is needed, when needed, in needed quantity. Eliminate waste. Requires stable demand, quality, suppliers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OEE calculation:
options: OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. Availability = (Loading - Downtime)/Loading
questions.solution: OEE: Loading: planned production time. Performance: (ideal cycle × output)/operating time. Quality: good/total.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Theil's U statistic:
questions.solution: Theil's U: measure of forecasting accuracy. U = 1: no better than naive. U = 0: perfect.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the control chart shown (CL = 50, UCL = 59, LCL = 41), the highlighted point lying above the UCL indicates that the process is:
options: In control with only chance variation
Out of control (an assignable cause is present)
questions.solution: A point beyond the ±3σ control limits (UCL = 59) signals an assignable cause — the process is out of statistical control.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 130" width="280" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="30" x2="260" y2="30" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="262" y="33" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">UCL</text><line x1="20" y1="70" x2="260" y2="70" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="262" y="73" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">CL</text><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="260" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="262" y="113" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">LCL</text><polyline points="40,68 70,60 100,75 130,55 160,18 190,72 220,66" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="40" cy="68" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><circle cx="70" cy="60" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><circle cx="100" cy="75" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><circle cx="130" cy="55" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><circle cx="160" cy="18" r="4" fill="#dc2626"/><circle cx="190" cy="72" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/><circle cx="220" cy="66" r="3" fill="#2563eb"/></svg>
figure.caption: Shewhart control chart

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 140" width="280" height="140"><circle cx="30" cy="70" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="30" y="74" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">1</text><circle cx="140" cy="30" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="140" y="34" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">2</text><circle cx="140" cy="110" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="140" y="114" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">3</text><circle cx="250" cy="70" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#2563eb"/><text x="250" y="74" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">4</text><line x1="42" y1="62" x2="128" y2="38" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="80" y="42" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">A=5</text><line x1="152" y1="36" x2="238" y2="62" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="200" y="42" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">B=6</text><line x1="42" y1="78" x2="128" y2="104" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="80" y="108" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">C=2</text><line x1="152" y1="104" x2="238" y2="78" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="200" y="108" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">D=10</text></svg>
figure.caption: CPM activity-on-arrow network

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Control phase tools:
options: SPC, control plan, mistake-proofing, standard work, training
questions.solution: Control: sustain gains. Statistical process control. Process documentation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — M/G/∞:
options: Infinite servers. No queue. L = λ·E[S]. Poisson arrivals. General service
questions.solution: M/G/∞: unlimited capacity. Self-service. Ex: library, parking lot. Poisson output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+8, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+5), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 13, 1-3-4 = 7. The critical path is the longest = 13 days.
